glossary.system-secure-storage: The platform-native credential store used by Nile when the user accepts OS-managed secret storage, such as macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager/DPAPI, or Linux Secret Service.
glossary.encrypted-local-storage: A Nile-managed local credential vault encrypted with a user-provided passphrase and stored in local files instead of the platform credential store.
glossary.credential-storage-mode: The single machine-level credential storage strategy active for one local Nile workspace instance; once any saved connection exists, the mode is locked until explicit reset.
glossary.legacy-credential-backend: Older per-connection backend metadata that may still exist for upgrade compatibility but is no longer the desired source of truth for new saves.

invariants.statement: Nile must not persist raw connection secrets or encrypted-local passphrases in SQLite, desktop preferences, logs, or test fixtures.
invariants.verification: Unit test credential persistence and desktop preference serialization so only backend metadata and credential references are stored; manually inspect the SQLite database and desktop preferences after creating connections.

invariants.statement: A local Nile workspace instance must use exactly one active credential storage mode for new saved credentials, and that mode becomes immutable until explicit reset once any saved connection exists.
invariants.verification: Unit test first-save mode selection, later-save reuse of the same mode, and reset-driven mode clearing; manually save one connection, attempt another save through a different surface, and verify the same mode is enforced.

invariants.statement: Encrypted local storage must require a user-provided passphrase and must never silently downgrade to plaintext local files or SQLite secret storage.
invariants.verification: Unit test encrypted-local credential writes to fail without a passphrase and to reject fallback-to-plaintext paths; manually deny system-secure storage and verify desktop only offers encrypted-local storage as the fallback.

invariants.statement: Portable credential export/import must build on encrypted local storage semantics and must not require product-level renaming or behavioral forks for OS-specific secure stores.
invariants.verification: Review core and surface specs to confirm `system_secure_storage` remains platform-abstract and `encrypted_local_storage` remains the portable vault foundation for future export/import work.
